titlestmt.titleproper: Free History Project Video Interviews with Jim Fouratt GEN MSS 1981
Free History Project Video Interviews with Jim Fouratt
titlestmt.author: Finding aid by Gabriela Redwine

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 1, 2022 at 03:58

did.unittitle: Free History Project video interviews with Jim Fouratt

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Consists of a video interview and research materials related to Don't Call Me Gay Zelig, a short film Sam Green of the Free History Project made about the gay activist Jim Fouratt. All digital files in the collection, including the images of items from earlier decades, are dated 2019.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Free History Project is a film production, distribution, and educational outreach non-profit organization.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Free History Project Video Interviews with Jim Fouratt. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subgroupings: Free History Project interview and Research Materials

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed to a preliminary level in 2020. Preliminary processing included listing of material at the top folder level. Descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section of this finding aid is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. File names have not been verified against the contents.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jim Fouratt (1941- ) is an American gay rights activist, writer, nightclub promoter, and music industry manager.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Free History Project Video Interviews with Jim Fouratt is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sam Green (1966- ) is an American documentary filmmaker.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of 5 video files and a transcript for the interview Sam Green conducted with Jim Fouratt.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted to reading-room access only. The files may not be duplicated for patron use or distributed online. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials in the collection include over 1,000 digitized photographs, clippings, and ephemera such as posters and buttons related to Fouratt's involvement in the gay rights movement, as well as a small amount of television footage (copies of .mp4 files) of Jim Fouratt. The Free History Project came across these items in the course of their research for the Fouratt interview.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted to reading-room access only. The files may not be duplicated for patron use or distributed online. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
